purpose: Aggregates state.diagnostics (Object<filePath, Array<{line,column,message,severity,source}>>) into a VS Code-style 'Problems' panel embedded in BottomPanel. Computes per-severity counts (error/warning/info, with hint mapped to info) and groups visible diagnostics by file path. Provides three filter toggle buttons (errors/warnings/info) that hide categories. Within a file, items sort by SEVERITY_ORDER (error<warning<info<hint), then by line, then by column. Each row shows SeverityIcon + message + optional source + [line:column]. Row click invokes onNavigate(filePath, line, column) prop — IDEView wires this to IDE_OPEN_FILE + ideNavigateToLine, opening the file in CodeEditor and revealing the line. shortenPath collapses by stripping prefix up to known project markers ('/src/', '/lib/', '/app/', '/pages/', '/components/', '/electron/').
gotchas: Diagnostics with severity 'hint' are rebucketed into 'info' — the original severity is lost in counts but preserved on the row's SeverityIcon (which renders the same info SVG anyway).
shortenPath markers are HARDCODED — projects without /src/, /lib/, etc. fall back to last-3-segments path. Custom layouts will look noisy.
useMemo dep is [diagnostics, activeFilters] — rebuilds on every diagnostics state change, which fires per-file via DIAGNOSTICS_SET (debounced 500ms upstream in CodeEditor).
Empty filter state shows 'All problems filtered out' — distinct from initial empty state ('No problems detected').
diagnostic.severity falsy → defaults to 'info' for sort/filter; SEVERITY_ORDER lookup falls through to 3 (treats as hint).
Row keys use `${filePath}-${line}-${column}-${idx}` — same diagnostic appearing twice on same line/column would still be unique via idx but visually duplicate.
patterns: Pure-derivation-from-state via useMemo — no local mutation of diagnostics.
SeverityIcon is severity-driven SVG: red circle for error, yellow triangle for warning, purple circle for info/hint.
Filter toggles are buttons with 'active' modifier class; clicking flips activeFilters[severity].
conventions: CSS class 'problems-' prefix for all elements.
Severity colors via CSS vars (var(--color-failed,#FF6B6B), var(--color-warning,#FFD93D), var(--color-in-progress,#9B7CF0)).
